original_abstract: Tabular data, structured in rows and columns, serves as the backbone of empirical research across domains as diverse as particle physics, biomedicine, and economics. Missing value prediction—specifically, inferring a target label from a set of features—is a fundamental task in these fields, underpinning everything from drug discovery to climate modeling. While deep learning has transformed our ability to process raw data such as images and text, gradient-boosted decision trees have remained the undisputed gold standard for tabular data for two decades. Here, we introduce the Tabular Prior-data Fitted Network (TabPFN), a tabular foundation model that shifts this paradigm. TabPFN outperforms existing methods by a wide margin on datasets with up to 10,000 samples, while requiring orders of magnitude less training time. In just 2.8 seconds, TabPFN surpasses the performance of an ensemble of state-of-the-art baselines tuned for four hours. As a generative transformer-based model, it supports a wide array of downstream tasks, including fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, density estimation, and the creation of reusable embeddings. Developed by training a meta-learning algorithm across millions of synthetic datasets, TabPFN demonstrates the transformative potential of learned algorithms. By significantly advancing modeling capabilities, TabPFN offers a powerful tool to accelerate scientific discovery and refine critical decision-making processes across the sciences.
humanized_abstract: Data presented in a table of rows and columns is ubiquitous in empirical studies of everything from subatomic particles and living cells to market trends and weather patterns. A ubiquitous data analysis task in such studies is the prediction of target variables from sets of input variables, which is encountered in for example drug development and climate modeling. Although deep learning has revolutionized analysis of image and text data in the past few years, gradient boosted decision trees have remained the leading methodology for tabular data for almost two decades. We here introduce a novel deep learning model called the Tabular Prior-data Fitted Network (TabPFN), which not only beats all other approaches on all of the commonly used benchmark datasets considered here (with up to 10 4 training examples), but at the same time learns several orders of magnitude faster. For instance, when competing against an ensemble of the current state of the art baseline models (obtained after hyper-parameter search for four hours), the training of our approach (run on a single GPU) only takes 2.8 seconds. In addition to classification, the TabPFN can be used for regression, synthetic to real data augmentation, density estimation and feature learning/hybrid representations. The model is obtained by training a meta-learning algorithm on millions of synthetic datasets, demonstrating in practice that learned algorithms can lead to vastly improved modeling of data. This in turn will greatly enhance speed of research and the accuracy of many of the numerous important scientific and business decisions that are made in tabular data environments.
